title: Plug & abandonment, at half the rig time.
intro: Complex and legacy wells with damaged casing, stuck packers or lost surface access can't be sealed conventionally. Gunnar drills a precise new path that intercepts the target wellbore — placing a permanent, regulator-witnessed plug where it counts. No-access ranging-while-drilling can cut typical P&A rig time by 50% or more.

heroStats.l: rig-time reduction vs. conventional wireline P&A
CTRWD™ tool size for tight, deep tubulars

challengeTitle: The hardest wells to abandon are the ones that matter most.
challenge: Many wells slated for abandonment have compromised integrity — collapsed casing, stuck tubing, or no usable surface access. Conventional methods rely on open-hole wireline runs that are slow, costly and carry well-control risk.
Old and unrecorded wells may have uncertain positions, and urban or offshore settings add access, environmental and regulatory pressure to every decision.
approachTitle: Intercept the well, seal it from the inside.
approach: Gunnar uses active magnetic ranging to drill a relief-style path that intercepts the target casing precisely, then mills in and places a deep cement plug on the inside — guaranteeing a permanent, durable abandonment.
No-access ranging-while-drilling eliminates wireline round-trips and open-hole exposure, removing kick and blowout risk while dramatically cutting rig time and cost.

capabilities.b: Intercept design, integrity assessment and regulatory documentation.
DeadSet™ and CTRWD™ locate the target without wireline exposure.
Outside-in milling to establish communication with the target casing.
Permanent plugs set inside the target wellbore with confidence.
Compact systems for rig-constrained, urban and offshore environments.
Documented, compliant abandonment — including CalGEM-certified projects.

tech.name: CTRWD™
tech.note: Coiled-tubing ranging-while-drilling — 3.5" tool deploys under pressure for deep, tight abandonment where rigs struggle.

tech.name: DeadSet™
tech.note: No-access wireline active ranging with ±1 ft accuracy — removes wireline kick and blowout risk.

tech.name: ClearCut™
tech.note: Precision milling simulation that converts intercept geometry into real-world milling parameters.

caseTitle: Urban Oil Well Abandonment — Los Angeles
caseBody: An old oil well in the heart of LA posed health and environmental hazards and blocked redevelopment. Gunnar's ranging and intercept technology permanently plugged the well and secured CalGEM certification — unlocking previously unusable land now slated for affordable housing.

faqs.q: How does ranging reduce P&A cost?
faqs.a: No-access ranging-while-drilling eliminates time-consuming open-hole wireline round-trips and the associated well-control risk. On complex P&A this can save roughly half the rig time and millions of dollars versus conventional methods.

faqs.q: Can you abandon wells with no surface access?
faqs.a: Yes. That is the core advantage of ranging-based P&A: by drilling a new path that intercepts the target wellbore underground, Gunnar can seal wells that are inaccessible, collapsed or unrecorded from surface.

faqs.q: Do you handle offshore and urban P&A?
faqs.a: Yes. Compact systems like the 3.5" CTRWD™ are suited to rig-constrained, urban and offshore settings, and Gunnar manages the environmental and regulatory requirements that come with them.

faqs.q: Is the abandonment permanent and compliant?
faqs.a: Plugs are placed inside the target casing and the work is regulator-witnessed. Gunnar has delivered CalGEM-certified abandonments approved for site redevelopment.
